title: Kudoa paradoxa
taxonomy: Kudoa paradoxa is a species of myxozoan parasites, specifically belonging to the family Multivalvulidae. The genus Kudoa includes several species of myxozoans, which are known to infect fish and other aquatic organisms. Kudoa paradoxa is typically found in the muscle tissue of infected fish, where it can cause significant economic losses due to reduced meat quality and potential health risks to consumers. This parasite is usually transmitted through the consumption of contaminated or undercooked fish. Kudoa paradoxa is considered a significant threat to global aquaculture and fisheries industries.
